title: Filing Readiness Agent
shortDescription: Pre-checks every filing for the formatting, signatures, exhibits, and certificates the court requires before the clerk rejects it.

seo.title: AI Filing Readiness Agent for Law Firms | Dodonai
seo.description: Pre-check every filing for formatting, signatures, exhibits, and certificates before the clerk rejects it. The two-day delay that disappears.

hero.headline: AI Filing Readiness Agent
hero.subheadline: Reviews every filing against the court's specific formatting, signature, exhibit, and certificate requirements before it goes to the clerk. The clerk-rejection loop that costs 2 days disappears.
bullets: Per-court formatting check (caption, font, margins, page limits)
Required signatures, certifications, and proof-of-service verification
Plain-English fix list before the filing goes to e-file

scenario.heading: The clerk rejection you would have absorbed
paragraphs: Your associate finishes a brief at 4pm on the day it's due and hits e-file at 4:45. The clerk rejects it the next morning at 9: the proof of service was missing the certified-mail tracking number, and exhibit B was referenced but not attached. The associate scrambles, refiles by 11. The Filing Readiness agent would have caught both issues before submission. Run it before e-file and the clerk-rejection round-trip stops happening.

whatItDoes.heading: What the Filing Readiness agent does
bullets: Verifies the filing matches the court's formatting requirements (caption format, font size, margins, page limits)
Checks for required signatures, attorney bar numbers, and certifications
Confirms proof of service is attached and complete
Cross-checks exhibit references against attached exhibits to catch missing or duplicated items
Produces a plain-English fix list before the filing is submitted

integrations: PACER, ECF, and state e-filing portals
Document management system for the draft and exhibit access
Practice management system for matter and venue context
Slack or email for the fix-list delivery

safety.heading: Safety posture
paragraphs: The agent doesn't file. It produces the fix list and waits for an attorney or paralegal to address the items before submission. For matters with a tight deadline where the fix list is empty, the agent confirms readiness; the actual e-file action is still a human click.

faq.question: What about courts with quirky local formatting requirements?
faq.answer: The per-court rule sets are encoded in skill files and updated as rules change. For unusual local requirements (a specific cover-sheet format, a redaction protocol, a chambers copy convention), the file captures them and the agent checks against them on every filing in that court.

faq.question: Does this work for paper filings or e-file only?
faq.answer: Both. For paper filings, the agent produces the same readiness check and adds verification of physical mailing requirements (chambers copies, judge's copies, courtesy copies). The check is the same; the action at the end is different.
